trivia_bullets.text: Aetherdrift (set code: DFT) released February 14, 2025, as MTG's 103rd expansion. The set is a multiplanar death-race spanning three planes: Avishkar (formerly Kaladesh, renamed to remove racial connotations), Amonkhet, and Muraganda. The race's prize is the Aetherspark — an artificial planeswalker spark created by an Avishkari inventor during the New Phyrexian Invasion [Scryfall: DFT set; magic.wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift Part 1].
sources: magic.wizards.com: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift Part 1

trivia_bullets.text: Outpace Oblivion carries the 'Start your engines!' keyword, Aetherdrift's core speed mechanic. Speed starts at 1 on first trigger and increases by 1 on each of your turns when an opponent loses life, capping at max speed (4). The card deals 5 damage on entry and can sacrifice itself for 2 damage to all players not at max speed — punishing opponents who fall behind the speed curve [Scryfall: card c22e415f].

Art by Raymond Swanland, a veteran Magic illustrator known for high-contrast, cinematic compositions. Outpace Oblivion was previewed January 30, 2025, via the official Wizards of the Coast YouTube channel. EDHREC rank 16838 indicates limited Commander demand — the card's strength is in Standard, not in singleton formats [Scryfall: artist Raymond Swanland; Scryfall: preview source YouTube link].
sources: Scryfall: card c22e415f — artist, preview fields

related_cards.name: Start Your Engines! // Max Speed
related_cards.note: Scryfall marks this as a combo_piece partner for Outpace Oblivion — the double-faced card that represents the speed mechanic itself
